claim: A DSPy GEPA-compiled program saved on Node A (Llama 3.1 8B Q4 + RTX 4090) and loaded on Node B (Qwen3 8B Q4 + Apple Silicon M4) preserves >=95% of its accuracy on the AIME math task. This is the structural foundation for OCM's v2+ skill federation network-effect lever per spec v0.3 row 23.
source_for_claim: DSPy GEPA paper: +10pp AIME, +17pp entity extraction. program.save()/program.load() is first-class. Spec v0.3 row 23 upgrade: federation lever.
comparison_anchor: optimization/dspy-gepa-same-node-baseline (compile + run on Node A, no transfer)
decision_rule: If CONFIRMED, signed compiled-skill artifacts are a viable v2+ federation primitive — proceed with mesh-distribution design. If REFUTED, GEPA programs are model-specific and federation-as-skill-sharing falls apart, forcing a different network-effect strategy.
blocked_on: DSPy GEPA optimizer not yet integrated into OCM
Two-node bench harness (presumes mesh transport from Phase 7+)
